# Rocinante

> A terminal cockpit for your agent fleet. Rocinante is a viewer. It reads status
> that agents already report and shows it in a keyboard-driven terminal UI. It
> never starts, stops, or commands agents.

Any agent joins the cockpit by reporting through one command. Nothing hand-writes
JSON, and nothing needs to know the file format, directory, or schema version.

## Report your status

Call the binary at the start and end of work:

```
rocinante report --id <id> --name <label> --kind <kind> --state <state> \
  --task "<one line>" --detail "<longer text>" --tokens <int>
```

- `--id` is required, stable, and unique. It becomes the filename.
- On a new agent, `--name`, `--kind`, and `--state` are required.
- A later call merges: it overwrites only the flags you pass, stamps the
  heartbeat, and resets the start time only when the state changes.
- `--kind` is one of: always-on, cron, launchd, claude-code, other.
- `--state` is one of: running, idle, blocked, error, offline.

## The Fleet Status Contract

One JSON file per agent lives at `~/.rocinante/fleet/<id>.json`. The bridge
watches that directory and updates instantly. The schema, version 1:

| Field          | Type    | Required | Notes                                          |
| -------------- | ------- | -------- | ---------------------------------------------- |
| `schema`       | int     | yes      | Contract version. The bridge refuses a newer major. |
| `id`           | string  | yes      | Stable, unique. The filename.                  |
| `name`         | string  | yes      | Human label.                                   |
| `kind`         | enum    | yes      | always-on, cron, launchd, claude-code, other.  |
| `state`        | enum    | yes      | running, idle, blocked, error, offline.        |
| `task`         | string  | no       | One line of current work.                      |
| `detail`       | string  | no       | Longer text, shown in the inspect view.        |
| `since`        | RFC3339 | yes      | When the current state began.                  |
| `heartbeat`    | RFC3339 | yes      | Last update. Drives staleness.                 |
| `tokens_today` | int     | no       | For agents that consume tokens.                |
| `meta`         | object  | no       | Freeform, such as pid or working directory.    |

Writes are atomic, a temp file plus a rename, so the bridge never reads a
half-written file.

## Derived state

The bridge infers silence. If now minus `heartbeat` exceeds the stale threshold,
the agent dims to stale. If it exceeds the offline threshold, the agent reads
offline. Defaults are 90 seconds and 300 seconds, set in `~/.rocinante/config.toml`.

## Claude Code sessions

Claude Code runs `rocinante hook` on each lifecycle event and passes the event as
JSON on stdin. The binary maps it to a report, so a live session appears with no
polling. See hooks/README.md.
